intro.title: Let's Verify This Event
intro.description: We're going to prove that this event data hasn't been changed since it was recorded on the blockchain.

steps.1: Look at the original event data
steps.2: See how it was encoded (CBOR format)
steps.4: Check that fingerprint matches what's in the CID
steps.5: Confirm the CID is stored on Algorand

intro.note: At each step, you can verify independently using external tools. No need to trust us.

eventData.title: Your Event Data
eventData.description: This is the information that was recorded for this event. It's stored as structured data (JSON).
eventData.note: This exact data is what we'll verify against the blockchain record.

cbor.title: Encoding to CBOR
cbor.description: The event data is converted to CBOR, a compact binary format. Think of it as a more efficient way to represent the same information.
cbor.verifyDescription: Open CBOR decoder to confirm the CBOR decodes back to the original JSON

hashing.title: Creating the Fingerprint
hashing.description: The CBOR data is run through SHA-256, a mathematical function that produces a unique "fingerprint". Any change to the data, even one character, would produce a completely different result.
hashing.verifyDescription: Use an independent SHA-256 calculator to verify
hashing.verifyHint: Set the input type to "Base64" before pasting the CBOR data

cid.title: The Content Identifier (CID)
cid.description: The hash is wrapped in a CID, a self-describing identifier that includes the hash plus metadata about how it was created.
cid.inspectTitle: When you inspect this CID, you'll find:
cid.hashMatch: The digest matches our calculated hash
cid.verifyDescription: Open CID Inspector to see the hash embedded in this identifier

blockchain.title: Stored on Algorand
blockchain.description: This CID was written to the Algorand blockchain in a transaction.

provides.immutability: Immutability: it can never be changed
provides.timestamp: Timestamp: proof of when it was recorded
provides.public: Public record: anyone can verify it

blockchain.verifyDescription: Open Algorand Explorer to see this transaction
blockchain.note: Look for the "Note" field in the transaction and switch its encoding to UTF-8. It contains our CID.

verified.title: Verification Complete
verified.description: This event data is authentic and hasn't been modified since it was anchored on {{date}}.
verified.learnMoreDescription: Read about how blockchain anchoring works

blockchainSection.description: These fields are the cryptographic proof that this event's data is authentic and unmodified.
